fields.description:
# Live-store remediation: drive fsck to zero (safe, reversible, batched, committed)

## Why
`rebar fsck` on the live store = 2422 SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT + 11 ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE. Fix #1
stopped new corruption; this cleans the backlog. DESTRUCTIVE on a live, git-backed shared store — safety is
first-class. Sibling deliverables are NEW work: A1 (tricolour-head-ratfish) ADDS the `.retired` rename +
`RETIRED_SUFFIX`; A2 (glandulous-potent-waxwing) ADDS the `include_retired` rebuild path. depends_on A1+A2.

## Repair per fault class
- SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT (2422): a folded source file STILL PRESENT (listed in `source_event_uuids`, never
  deleted). Repair = retire (`.retired`) each still-present listed source. The retire helper is invoked from the
  `fsck --repair` path (shared with A1's rename logic). NOT a rebuild.
- ORPHAN_EVENT (11): a pre-snapshot event absent from `source_event_uuids` -> A2's `reduce_ticket(..., include_retired=True)` rebuild -> re-write snapshot.
- MISSING_CREATE (1): `fsck --repair` logs it and routes to human triage (no automatic write).

## Safety
- GIT-AWARE writes: the tracker is a git worktree. Each batch of per-ticket repairs is committed+pushed:
  `git -C <tracker> add -A && git -C <tracker> commit -m 'a3-remediation: batch N/M' && git -C <tracker> push`.
  The pre-remediation restore point and rollback are git-level and cover these commits: BEFORE any write,
  `git -C <tracker> tag pre-a3-remediation <oid>`; rollback = `git -C <tracker> reset --hard pre-a3-remediation
  && git -C <tracker> push --force-with-lease`.
- Dry-run FIRST: `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after diff + dispositions, writes NOTHING
  (a test asserts the dry-run makes zero file writes and zero commits); the live run is gated on human review.
- Reconciler pause (real mechanism; `OBSERVE` is NOT a Mode): disable the `reconcile-bridge.yml` GHA schedule for
  the window (primary) — the pass-lock `refs/reconciler/lock` is a leased CAS lock (ADR 0031) and would expire, so
  it is NOT the pause mechanism. Verify no pass is mid-flight before starting; re-enable the schedule after. Manual
  writers hold off during the window (documented); because enforcement is social, the batched+committed design
  means a stray concurrent write merges rather than being lost, and the pre-tag enables rollback.
- Idempotent/resumable: the AUTHORITATIVE resumability check is fsck itself (re-run repairs only tickets fsck still
  flags). A per-ticket `.a3-repaired` marker is a LOCAL, gitignored optimization only (never committed) so a git
  rollback need not track it; a stale marker after rollback is harmless (fsck re-derives). A crash between the
  snapshot rename and the marker write is safe (fsck re-checks). Per-snapshot replace is atomic (write `<snap>.tmp`,
  `os.rename` over old); per-ticket failure logs+skips (no batch abort). Batched `--limit=200`, wait >=30s between.

## Orphan disposition policy
- AUTO-RECOVER (additive): COMMENT, LINK, UNLINK, TAG_DELTA, COMMITS.
- HUMAN TRIAGE (LWW-scalar; order unrecoverable): STATUS, EDIT (title/description/priority/assignee/parent),
  FILE_IMPACT, VERIFY_COMMANDS, SIGNATURE (the top-level signature mirror). Decision recorded in the PR / ops doc.

## Scope
`_commands/fsck.py` (`--repair`/`--dry-run`/`--limit` + per-batch git commit + local marker + atomic per-snapshot
rewrite + retire-still-present), `_commands/compact.py` (shared retire helper). Consumes A1's `.retired` + A2's
`include_retired`. Gated on RC1 fix #1 (deployed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after + dispositions; a test asserts ZERO file writes and ZERO git commits in dry-run.
- [ ] Live repair commits+pushes each batch to the tickets branch; pre-remediation tag created; rollback (`reset --hard` + `--force-with-lease`) documented AND rehearsed on a fixture (asserts post-rollback store == pre-tag state).
- [ ] Reconciler paused by disabling the GHA schedule (verified no in-flight pass); re-enabled after.
- [ ] Resumability authoritative via fsck re-check; `.a3-repaired` marker is local/gitignored; a test verifies a re-run skips already-clean tickets and that a crash-before-marker is safe.
- [ ] Synthetic-fixture unit tests: known SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT / ORPHAN_EVENT / MISSING_CREATE (incl. `.retired`) repair correctly; per-snapshot replace is atomic.
- [ ] Live store reaches 0 UNRESOLVED faults (triaged+documented orphans count as resolved); before/after counts recorded.
